sentences.text: OK.

During the time when we noticed, and they let us know-- actually loud with-- through the loudspeakers-- that anyone who is willing to leave and go to the-- to Russia to work, so they will-- they have the rights to meet there and there.
And there will be trucks with no charge.
And so I was then pregnant already, so I refused to go.
And Harry went.
And we-- yes.
Yes, that was at the approval of both of us.
OK.
By coming home-- back home, I-- I stand up to-- to the mezuzah, and I was very crying.
And I said, I have a such a good opportunity, but shall I go back?
Well, God gave me the good idea.
What-- what will be if somebody will grab me?
And I'm not supposed to go anymore.
So I saw-- noticed that's the old man-- the-- the landlord was an old man.
He was saying already, what do we say before being buried?
Kit-- kit?
Kaddish?
No, no, no.
He was saying-- before a person thinks that he is going to die.
You know, there is a pray-- I forgot already.
I'll remind myself.
I mean, it's in-- this is not important.
But he felt, you know, to believe me, that's-- you know.
So-- and then I thought to myself, what can I lose?
If I'll go, we will get killed.
I'll take the baby, and I'll go again.
Maybe he thought it over not to listen to his mother.
Maybe his mother decided to accept.
So I took the baby fast, and I-- and I-- he was just a few weeks old-- in a pillow, and a linen.
And I, you know, I band him around.
I don't know.
Maybe a little bottle I took probably, with-- whatever there were.
And-- but here is already-- I hear people are going.
They started to walk They're crying!
And it got dark.
And I looked out through the window.
I said, look.
Did the 5 o'clock passed already?
Ooh, I shall rush!
God knows what will be.
And I went up and prayed.
[NON-ENGLISH].
I said, like, we have a pray.
God shall ascend [NON-ENGLISH], you know, the angels.
[NON-ENGLISH] is in the right side and the [NON-ENGLISH].
And we left the [NON-ENGLISH] with the name is [NON-ENGLISH].
[NON-ENGLISH] that is our name.
And on the back is [NON-ENGLISH] and-- but, yeah.
OK.
And a [NON-ENGLISH]-- above my head.
[NON-ENGLISH] It means the-- the God shall cover me up.
You know, he shall-- he shall give me these instructions.
You know, it's also goes into that.
And I took my baby.
And I thought, well, I'll go, but I won't be going.
I have to escape.
I have to run away from here.
Here I saw the-- the snow was-- was, you know, falling on my face-- the forest snow-- November 5, 19-- 1942.
And I-- and hear little boychik.
He goes, mama, mama, where are you?
Daddy, papa, where are you?
Where are you?
They lost each other already!
And here we have the Banderists with the, you know, the-- the Ukraines.
They were such a murderers-- and the-- and the Germany army.
Everybody was watching us we shall not run away or something.
So I thought to myself, que sera.
You know, whatever will be, will be.
And I just started, like, to walk away like this.
I just walked away.
I just walked away.
And I was-- I was-- it was near a-- near a building already-- you see, near some building.
I thought to myself, if I'll just-- just pass this little piece, then I'll run into this building.
And this is what had happened.
I walked over there.
And I got in.
And I was waiting until the end, until the end from this-- this site where I-- where I am-- and-- where I was.
And then I-- I walked.
I walked up to-- to him-- to [NON-ENGLISH] Street.
I don't know.
The-- the angels were watching me.
Right above me they watch.
It's a miracle from God.
And here, when I came over to her, she-- the mother was already outside, and the two children.
And he was-- that was like a platform, you know, to walk up like two small steps.
So I didn't walked up to him.
And she was, oh, [NON-ENGLISH].
She was thinking I came to go together with her-- with them.
So I was like this standing near that platform, and he was on the platform.
And I was holding the baby.
And she said, [NON-ENGLISH]!
They'll kill us before coming there.
I didn't say nothing.
She walked away with the children, and he remained on the platform.
And I said, Mr.
Geller, do you really-- I-- I just touched his boots-- the end of-- like-- like the front of his boots.
I said, Mr.
Geller, do you really want me to leave?
Two you're going-- it's-- it's in front of you that you are going to save two lives, especially a young life, like my baby.
I have no where else to go.
You have a [NON-ENGLISH].
And exactly, your wife is in Russia.
I'll only stay for over the control.
I didn't go to bed.
Who could-- could even?
I just put my baby to bed, you know.
So he said to me, give me the baby.
He gave it to me.
He said, go this way.
And I walked up on the second floor where they were living.
And he saved my-- the first beginning of my life saving was him.
And there-- And then where did you go from?
So early in the morning, I didn't have no [NON-ENGLISH] during a-- a control.
I had nothing to-- to prove.
And he told me that.
He told me that, what will you do during control?
I said, I'll think of something.
Whatever will be, will be.
So early in the morning, before 8 o'clock, a army man came to call for him.
This is what he always used to be called to go to war.
Because he-- he worked for the [NON-ENGLISH] to make a [NON-ENGLISH] in his apartments.
And this is why his back jacket was-- [LAUGHS] --help me!
[LAUGHS] I'm laughing now.
You don't know what I went through.
You-- you know.
And here he came in-- came up.
And I said to him in German, [NON-ENGLISH].
My mind goes-- goes-- you know, with-- be working.
[NON-ENGLISH].
I-- I know German and how to.
We did took up in school, the German, too-- then changed to French as a-- as a extra, you know, additional language-- foreign language.
So I said, I don't have what to show.
And they went.
I will-- he will-- he hadn't [NON-ENGLISH] the proof whether when-- when the, you know, control [NON-ENGLISH].
[NON-ENGLISH].
So he ask me, what shall I do?
I said, you can-- you can do a lot for me and with a little bit of work.
What is it?
I said, when you will come to the [NON-ENGLISH] can you make a copy for me?
And I asked him.
The [NON-ENGLISH] is a real-- is a nice man.
He shall only sign on the copy that he's approved. approving the copy.
I said, [NON-ENGLISH].
Oh, this they said.
[NON-ENGLISH].
This is a [NON-ENGLISH].
I mean, he will-- he will try, you know, to help me.
He went away.
And I was thinking to myself, God help me.
I said this.
Up until now it'll be my turn!
They-- he shall-- he shall do my, you know, meet my request.
It didn't take too long.
He did come back.
He gave me [NON-ENGLISH].
And he said to me-- taught me how I shall lock the doors and not to open for no one the doors-- at least that they will break it.
You see, even among them, some, you know, some were good.
Some were good-- some angel, maybe [NON-ENGLISH].
You know what was [NON-ENGLISH]?
One among thousand.
OK.
So I had control.
Two SS people came in and two Ukrainians with the-- with the riffles.
All wonder where I at.
That's it.
This is here.
I am hiding myself with a baby.
So-- and the [NON-ENGLISH], I put it under my pillow, see?
And the baby started to cry.
I'm telling you.
So when I took out, it was just a paper.
The other one had a form, you know, from the office.
This was only a copy.
No, no, no, he said.
I am [NON-ENGLISH].
I-- I am trying to fool, you know.
So I-- so I said to him in Ukrainian-- because I was born in Ukraine-- Poland and Ukraine.
So I said to him, read it!
So he said, come with us.
So I said to the SS man, [NON-ENGLISH].
These are the alphabet.
These people, they are alphabet.
They don't know to read German.
Please read it.
So he took it over from my hands, and he read it.
He said, that's good.
It said, it's sufficient.
It's enough.
Let's go.
I had twice.
I had twice-- two times-- even the-- that's what I-- then when they are day-- was the second day was just-- and the-- and the Judenrein.
When he came back from work, he said, you know what we have now?
And we saw it through the windows, [NON-ENGLISH].
The way such a cultural country took upon themselves to-- to destroy a nation from 6 million Jews.
Why?
Why did they do it?
What wrong did we do?
We're decent people, educated people, obey the rules, pay the taxes.
Why?
Must be a reason.
No any other reason according to my mind-- Hitler was a maniac.
And this is what the people didn't understand.
And that is the reason when he felt a little bit better, he found out to kill, to take suicide.
And he suicided.
But where-- where was the world?
After that, we went-- when he came from work, he told us we have to go a [NON-ENGLISH].
They prepared and made it very fast.
They said in-- in-- if any Jews remained, they shall go there.
It's already prepared-- you know, closed up, where there was a needle wires.
A ghetto.
[NON-ENGLISH] is in Polish, called [NON-ENGLISH].
But a ghetto-- ghetto in English.
And everybody who remained has to go there.
And then they shall come to the Judenrat and register themselves.
They shall register also if any children remained so they're taken to a kindergarten in order the parents shall be able to work.
He came over, and he told me this story.
So I said, well, we will go to the [NON-ENGLISH].
So he said, and?
I said, and I will not go to the Judenrat.
I will not sign it, and I will not give up my child to-- I do not believe them.
I wish the people would not believe them!
Before they walked through the [NON-ENGLISH], each and every one could find a place where to hide himself.
But it wasn't.
When we were in [NON-ENGLISH], I still didn't believe them.

I was thinking to myself, if they will come through the ghetto, and see whether I have a child, and I didn't return it through the-- to them, to a kindergarten-- what, all of a sudden they want good-- a kindergarten?
So this is what I said.
So I decided to go to a-- to a goyish-- to a-- a non-Jewish section where I shall try to find someone who is willing to trust me, and I shall be able to trust them.
I shall pay them later, and they shall take me to Mlynow, to my sister's house.
Because this I felt that Mlynow-- first of all, it's my sister who lives there with her family.
And there, maybe until they will be able to-- this is what everybody used to say.
They are killing, because they made the active-- first, because this is a large city, Rovno But if-- if Mlynow is a small one, so let me try-- try, I thought to myself.
I'm trying to convince myself that there won't happen what happened in Rovno.
So by going-- so I-- I ripped up my-- I removed my-- the Magen David.
And I put it on a white 'scurf'-- scarf-- like this-- you know, like this, that I am sick on my eye.
They shall not recognize my Jewish eyes.
And I walked through the-- in the-- the goyish streets, the [NON-ENGLISH], we used to have.
Here as I walk, I've noticed a [NON-ENGLISH], a-- a wagon.
And an old man walked out the minute he noticed me.
It was wintertime, as I said.
You know, it was very cold-- with a long [NON-ENGLISH], they used to call it-- a long winter coat.
And he said like this to me.
So I thought, well, who know?
Maybe [NON-ENGLISH].
I always believed in God.
Someone has-- has-- this is what I prayed for otherwise.
I walked over, and he said, what are you looking for?
What [NON-ENGLISH]?
What are you looking for?
What are-- so I said, I'm looking for a [NON-ENGLISH] you know, to take me to Mlynow.
And I'll pay 15,000 ruble.
And I'll give you my husband's [NON-ENGLISH] and this, [NON-ENGLISH] boots-- leather boots.
Yeah, he left them anyhow.
He said, all right.
[NON-ENGLISH].
You know-- [LAUGHS]
--I will take you.
I said, you will?
I'm Jewish-- like this.
He said, this is OK.
[NON-ENGLISH].
Everything was [NON-ENGLISH].
[NON-ENGLISH] is OK.
[NON-ENGLISH].
So he said, but you have to come.
I'm not going now.
I have a baby, and I-- I would like you to come early in the morning-- early in the morning before the-- the sun raises up.
And I'll show you where to come, because I live in-- in the [NON-ENGLISH].
He said, [NON-ENGLISH].
I shall sit down on his wagon, and he'll go with me.
I shall show him where I live, where to pick me up.
And by the way, I told him, I asked him if it's possible he should bring me.
We will exchange.
He shall bring me the-- you know, blanket covers of his, with red stripes.
And I'll give him mine-- white, white, and white.
You know what I mean?
No?
Well, OK.
I-- I told you.
We-- we decided that I am-- I am his-- yeah, OK.
I will be like his daughter, and the child was sick.
Now we are from the hospital.
But how come to the hospital and I going with white [NON-ENGLISH]?
You know, white bath-- you know, the-- Bed linens.
--bedding, yeah, bedding.
So I decided to ask him.
There's the Ukrainian-- used to be with stripes, red stripes, like this, you know.
So I said, could you?
And I'll give you mine.
And bring me a white [NON-ENGLISH], your wife's.
Bring me another scarf.
I have to put on on the top this scarf, the other scarf.
[NON-ENGLISH].
Everything he accepted.
And I swear to God that this is what I'm telling you is the honest truth.
It's no story-- no false story.
It's a real and true story.
He came over.
And he came through the wires into the-- exactly to the apartment.
And when I came back, I told him.
I told Mr.
Geller, and I told the-- the main, you know, the owner of that building.
And there was already his brother who was hidden there, which I was responsible for them when during the control, the second control.
They ask me, who was in-- more Jews?
And I said, no.
No.
And instead to-- to lead them through the kitchen, I led them through another room.
But because I knew that in the kitchen they will be talking.
They don't know if someone is here.
So then they'll kill us-- all of us.
Anyways, I went to [NON-ENGLISH].
So I saved a few-- three people.
I saved-- there were three people.
I just gave them a little signal they should be quiet when I hear downstairs the doors were broken in.
We-- we made that agreement.
If any touch, I'll give it, you know, with the broom.
They shall know.
OK.
He came into my house.
So this woman said to me, who knows who he is?
Who know where he will take you?
He could kill you, take all of your things.
He'll kill you with the child and ahead of the time.
But she has a suggestion.
Her brother owned a-- a manufacture where they make soda water, all those cold drinks.
So he's not too far.
Now he works for the Germans.
Before it was his own.
But now he is there, she said.
Go, go.
He starts at 6 o'clock in the morning to-- to work for the Germans.
So how can I go?
How shall I ask?
I said, I can't ask him for his name.
And Geller said to him-- Shmulak Geller said to him, do you have a passport?
He said, yes.
He sticked his hands.
He took out the passport, and he showed him.
He showed him who he is, his name, and where he lives.
But how does he know?
He doesn't know, you know.
So she said, take the passport and go to my brother.
This is how much they respected me.
They were interested like I would be one of their family.
I wish they-- they would be alive and I shall meet him.
She was Feldman.
Her name was Feldman.
But I don't remember the first name.
And so I went there.
And I came, and he looked-- the brother.
And I told him the story.
The sister send me, because I have an opportunity to go to my sister.
And-- and your sister said that I shall go and ask you if know people.
He said-- he read it.
He said, [NON-ENGLISH].
From [NON-ENGLISH].
He was-- the name was-- I can't ever forget it.
[NON-ENGLISH].
You want to trust him?
You might as well sit with them-- like this.
So I went back.
And I thought to myself, why do I-- I was taught by my parents not to listen what someone will suggest to you.
Do your feeling.
Then you'll know whether you've done right or wrong.
She was a very intelligent person.
And I said to him, I decided to [NON-ENGLISH].
I decided I'm going to-- to go.
And I had a few suitcases with me-- after the killing.
After the action, we-- I went with him to my house, and we took our two suitcases here-- two together.
So there this old man took the suitcases to help me to carry it.
He shall bring me, and he did-- took a long time.
When we came home to my sister, it was already nighttime.
This is how long it is to drive.
By the way, on the way, he drove over to a-- how do you call that?
It's like a-- where they used to take water.
From-- A well.
--well-- a well-- to a well.
And the-- the women from near the well from their houses ran out.
And he knew them.
He said to me, go and give your baby.
You have to give-- feed your baby.
That goy said to me.
So I thought to myself, you know, I got hot-- hot and cold.
But then I thought to myself, maybe he's right.
Maybe he is a good man.
You know, maybe something is in his mind to help a one Jew, you know.
Maybe he killed a lot of them.
He wanted to help out one Jew.
This is what it was in my mind.
And I walked down.
And I-- oye, the baby never was so wet, and [NON-ENGLISH], and what-- whatever.
The women walked after me, and they saw him taking care of my [NON-ENGLISH].
What I-- I covered them up, you know, they shall not see.
But I am saying it, telling the truth.
And I gave them whatever.
And then this other from there, he went to the kitchen.
And he brought over to me-- I shall drink-- you know, like a cup.
They have different kind of a cups.
I shall drink milk and give the baby a little bit with a-- with a little spoon, a wooden spoon.
I shall give the baby.
You see what a goy can do?
One man can do-- can help-- but if-- if we don't give up.
We have to just try, try until the last minute.
And don't give in!
Don't give in.
And I, and I [NON-ENGLISH].
[NON-ENGLISH]-- yeah-- [LAUGHS] --in Hebrew.
So when I came to my sister's window, they were already resting in the bed.
And-- and when my sister heard my voice, she said to-- to my brother-in-law, Yitzhak, that was Mandelker, you know, which you saw.
I think I hear Molly's voice.
So he said, this is a dream.
Why did he say that?
Because she send a man to Rovno.
She paid him-- a goy.
He shall go to the Judenrat and find out about me.
But when he came, he came.
He came to my house.
I wasn't there.
God knows who was there.
And he went to the Judenrat.
They told him if I'm not there.
And I am not here.
So it's me that I am-- I went there where all the Jews went.
So he came to her, and he told her.
So she was sitting shiva.
She-- my sister was-- she showed me.
She said, you see, this [NON-ENGLISH].
And we were so crying.
It was [NON-ENGLISH].
It was-- it was just unbelievable, you know.
And I said to my brother-in-law, take care of-- of him.
He had done like [NON-ENGLISH].
So he took him to the other room.
I said, give him as much as he will ask.
So he gave him.
He gave this man.
And he came, this man, he took my two hands, and he kissed me.
He kissed them.
I gave him [NON-ENGLISH].
I trusted him.
You see?
And then what happened after that?
Did you stay in Mlynow with Pearl and Yitzhak or--
Well I stayed in with my sister not too long.
I felt that it's impossible that it won't happen here.
Excuse me.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Excuse me-- from talking long.
[DRINKING] I didn't believe.
I didn't believe.
My sister didn't believe.
You know, they came in, and-- and neighbors and this.
And-- and, you know.
So I said to-- and then they made it with [NON-ENGLISH] there.
The-- a-- a--
--a ghetto there.
And there they said, if anyone from the women wants to, they shall-- they shall go to work.
So I said to-- to my sister, you know something?
As long as you are here, you will take care of Shayala, and I'll go to work.
So it's-- it was-- there was a-- a-- but I don't remember.
It wasn't near.
It wasn't too far away from Mlynow.
I should find out maybe sometime.
There is somebody who lives in Tel Aviv-- in-- in New York, who was with me together in the same woods, you know.
So-- so I went to work, and what did I receive?
Two pieces of bread and a potato.
And this is what I could bring home.
Then I said to my sister, you see?
You see what kind of a?
Why should I go to work?
I'll take the baby, and I'll go to the Czechoslovakian-- there was a colony, like a-- in the farms.
It was owned by Czech people but born probably in Poland.
They were very rich.
And my sister and I knew that my sister told me she gave them so many things to hide.
You know, if anyone will remain, I shall know that they have them.
Schultz-- you have the name-- Schultz-- Mr-- family Schultz.
We used to--
